claim: Genomic instability in leukemia cells results from an increase in reactive oxygen species from oncogene activation.

result.claim: Genomic instability in leukemia cells results from an increase in reactive oxygen species from oncogene activation.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by the evidence. Paper ID 13023410 explicitly states that the "oncogenic BCR/ABL tyrosine kinase induces constitutive DNA damage in Philadelphia chromosome (Ph)-positive leukemia cells" and that "BCR/ABL-induced reactive oxygen species (ROSs) cause chronic oxidative DNA damage resulting in double-strand breaks (DSBs)." It further proposes a mechanism: "BCR/ABL --> ROSs --> oxidative DNA damage --> DSBs in proliferating cells --> unfaithful HRR and NHEJ repair," directly linking oncogene activation to increased ROS and subsequent genomic instability in leukemia cells. Paper ID 5765455 corroborates this by showing a "concomitant increase in reactive oxygen species (ROS) in transgenic mice with disease progression" in a myeloid leukemia model, and links "gene abnormalities to constitutive DNA damage and increased DSB repair errors in vivo." It also notes that RAC1, an essential component of ROS-producing NADPH oxidase, is downstream of RAS, an oncogene. Paper ID 1941721 provides general support for the link between ROS and genomic instability, demonstrating that "elevating the level of reactive oxygen species by overexpressing the antioxidant enzyme superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1), in a transgenic mouse, increases chromosome breakage." While the papers have 0 citations, their abstracts provide direct and consistent evidence for the claim.

evidence.text: The BCR/ABL tyrosine kinase induces constitutive DNA damage in Philadelphia chromosome (Ph)-positive leukemia cells.
evidence.source: BCR/ABL oncogenic kinase promotes unfaithful repair of the reactive oxygen species-dependent DNA double-strand breaks.

evidence.text: The effects of SOD1 on CK1γ stability are observed in a human cell line.
evidence.source: SOD1 Integrates Signals from Oxygen and Glucose to Repress Respiration

evidence.text: Elevating the level of reactive oxygen species by overexpressing the antioxidant enzyme superoxide dismutase 1 (SOD1) increased chromosome breakage in a transgenic mouse.
evidence.source: Oxygen Metabolism Causes Chromosome Breaks and Is Associated with the Neuronal Apoptosis Observed in DNA Double-Strand Break Repair Mutants

evidence.text: Tspan3 deletion impaired leukemia stem cell self-renewal.
evidence.source: Tetraspanin 3 Is Required for the Development and Propagation of Acute Myelogenous Leukemia.

evidence.text: Eliminating BCL-2 yielded rapid loss of leukemic cells.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: PDS causes deregulated G2/M progression in HR-defective cells.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds

evidence.text: Mitochondrial dysfunction in primary mitochondrial diseases promotes reactive oxygen species production.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial Dynamics in Mitochondrial Diseases

evidence.text: PDS induces DSB accumulation in HR-defective cells.
evidence.source: Targeting BRCA1 and BRCA2 Deficiencies with G-Quadruplex-Interacting Compounds

evidence.text: BCR/ABL-induced reactive oxygen species (ROSs) cause chronic oxidative DNA damage.
evidence.source: BCR/ABL oncogenic kinase promotes unfaithful repair of the reactive oxygen species-dependent DNA double-strand breaks.

evidence.text: DUSP4 overexpression increased chemotherapy-induced apoptosis.
evidence.source: Profiling of residual breast cancers after neoadjuvant chemotherapy identifies DUSP4 deficiency as a mechanism of drug resistance

evidence.text: The mice developed lymphoblastic leukemia.
evidence.source: Antiapoptotic BCL-2 is required for maintenance of a model leukemia.

evidence.text: Mitochondria are a major source of reactive oxygen species (ROS) in the signaling response to a more oxidized extracellular E(h)CySS.
evidence.source: A key role for mitochondria in endothelial signaling by plasma cysteine/cystine redox potential.

evidence.text: Nac1 overexpression promotes ESC proliferation and delays ESC differentiation in the absence of leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF).
evidence.source: Nac1 promotes self-renewal of embryonic stem cells through direct transcriptional regulation of c-Myc

evidence.text: There is a concomitant increase in reactive oxygen species (ROS) in transgenic mice with disease progression.
evidence.source: Reactive oxygen species, DNA damage, and error-prone repair: a model for genomic instability with progression in myeloid leukemia?

evidence.text: Mitochondria are the main source of reactive oxygen species.
evidence.source: New Data on Effects of SkQ1 and SkQT1 on Rat Liver Mitochondria and Yeast Cells

evidence.text: MDS has an increased propensity to develop acute myelogenous leukemia (AML).
evidence.source: Reactive oxygen species, DNA damage, and error-prone repair: a model for genomic instability with progression in myeloid leukemia?

evidence.text: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) is an aggressive hematological tumor resulting from the malignant transformation of lymphoid progenitors.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL

evidence.text: SOD1 promotes kinase stability by catalyzing superoxide conversion to peroxide.
evidence.source: SOD1 Integrates Signals from Oxygen and Glucose to Repress Respiration

evidence.text: The effect of SOD1 on chromosome breakage can be modulated by cellular oxygen tension.
evidence.source: Oxygen Metabolism Causes Chromosome Breaks and Is Associated with the Neuronal Apoptosis Observed in DNA Double-Strand Break Repair Mutants

evidence.text: Each 5 kg/m(2) increase in BMI was associated with a hazard ratio of 1·09 (1·05-1·13; p≤0·0001) for leukaemia.
evidence.source: Body-mass index and risk of 22 specific cancers: a population-based cohort study of 5·24 million UK adults
